from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple template mapping for the most common predicates.
    # More predicates can be added later.
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{s} has a {o} style",
        "buildingStartDate": "{s} started construction in {o}",
        "completionDate": "{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": "{s} cost {o}",
        "floorArea": "{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": "{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{s} is a {o}",
        "developer": "{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "{s} is tenanted by {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": "{s} has the address {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{s} was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": "{s} is in the {o} region",
        "state": "{s} is in {o}",
        "website": "{s} website is {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{s} was built in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": (
            "{s} has the NRHP reference number {o}"
        ),
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": (
            "{s} was added to the National Register of Historic Places on {o}"
        ),
        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "chancellor": "{s} has chancellor {o}",
        "governmentType": "{s} has a government type of {o}",
        "capital": "{s} has capital {o}",
        "language": "{s} speaks {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has a leader titled {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the currency {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
        "origin": "{s} originated from {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is known for the project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} people are called {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} contains {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{s} has key person {o}",
        "architecture": "{s} features {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=t.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic phrasing
            clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence using commas and a final conjunction.
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence_body = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    # Ensure proper punctuation.
    sentence = sentence_body.rstrip(".") + "."
    # Capitalize first character.
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
